xml.etree.ElementInclude/XMLParser.name: XMLParser
xml.etree.ElementInclude/XMLParser.description: This class is the low-level building block of the module. It uses xml.parsers.expat for efficient, event-based parsing of XML. It can be fed XML data incrementally with the feed() method, and parsing events are translated to a push API - by invoking callbacks on the target object. If target is omitted, the standard TreeBuilder is used. If encoding [1] is given, the value overrides the encoding specified in the XML file. Changed in version 3.8: Parameters are now keyword-only. The html argumen...

xml.etree.ElementInclude/XMLPullParser.name: XMLPullParser
xml.etree.ElementInclude/XMLPullParser.description: A pull parser suitable for non-blocking applications. Its input-side API is similar to that of XMLParser, but instead of pushing calls to a callback target, XMLPullParser collects an internal list of parsing events and lets the user read from it. events is a sequence of events to report back. The supported events are the strings "start", "end", "comment", "pi", "start-ns" and "end-ns" (the “ns” events are used to get detailed namespace information). If events is omitted, only "end" events are...

xml.etree.ElementInclude/XML.name: XML
xml.etree.ElementInclude/XML.description: Parses an XML section from a string constant. This function can be used to embed “XML literals” in Python code. text is a string containing XML data. parser is an optional parser instance. If not given, the standard XMLParser parser is used. Returns an Element instance.

xml.etree.ElementInclude/XMLID.name: XMLID
xml.etree.ElementInclude/XMLID.description: Parses an XML section from a string constant, and also returns a dictionary which maps from element id:s to elements. text is a string containing XML data. parser is an optional parser instance. If not given, the standard XMLParser parser is used. Returns a tuple containing an Element instance and a dictionary.
